title: crotalaria subcapitata subsp. oreadum
intro: Crotalaria subcapitata subsp. oreadum, also known as the mountain rattlebox or mountain crotalaria, is a perennial plant species in the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of South Africa. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which are attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Crotalaria subcapitata subsp. oreadum include its potential use in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and protect against oxidative stress. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
